skill.name: c4-architecture-c4-architecture
skill.description: Generate C4 architecture documentation for an existing repository using bottom-up code analysis.
skill.summary: Creates C4 code, component, container, and context documentation from a repository.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown examples, C4 terminology, documentation links, and diagram instructions. No prompt injection, malicious code execution, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found. The remaining risk is low because the workflow intentionally writes generated documentation files into the repository.

low_findings.title: Intended Repository File Writes
low_findings.description: The workflow directs the assistant to create C4-Documentation files in the repository. This is expected behavior, but users should review generated documentation before committing it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-write instructions are explicit and repeated, but they are limited to documentation outputs rather than executable code or hidden locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Detected as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged inline code spans and a fenced usage example as shell or Ruby backtick execution. The reviewed file is Markdown instructions and does not contain executable script logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown formatting, configuration option names, an output tree, or a literal example command. No runtime command execution API or script file is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Links Detected as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to public C4 model documentation. The skill does not instruct the assistant to fetch, upload, or transmit repository data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are ordinary Markdown references to c4model.com. There is no network client, webhook, remote API call, or data transfer instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches C4 Documentation Terms
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts correspond to ordinary prose such as names, descriptions, signatures, diagrams, and documentation sections. No cryptographic algorithm or security implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review found C4 documentation vocabulary and Markdown structure at the flagged lines, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Diagram and Search Guidance Detected as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to Mermaid diagram guidance and repository documentation discovery steps. These are normal architecture analysis instructions for this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines request diagrams or local repository discovery for documentation. No host fingerprinting, credential discovery, or external enumeration behavior is described.

content.value_statement: Architecture knowledge is often scattered across code, deployment files, and informal notes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured C4 documentation workflow.
actual_capabilities: Guides bottom-up analysis from code directories to system context.
Defines outputs for C4 code, component, container, and context documentation.
Prompts for function signatures, classes, dependencies, interfaces, and source links.
Includes Mermaid diagram guidance for component, container, and context views.
Requests OpenAPI 3.1 specifications for discovered container APIs.
Organizes generated files under a C4-Documentation directory.
limitations: Requires access to the target repository and enough context to inspect source files.
Generated architecture docs may need review by system owners for accuracy.
Large repositories can require many model calls and significant processing time.
Referenced subagent names require a compatible environment or manual adaptation.

use_cases.title: Document a Legacy System
use_cases.description: Create a navigable C4 documentation set for a repository with limited existing architecture notes.

use_cases.title: Prepare Architecture Reviews
use_cases.description: Generate component and container views that help teams discuss boundaries, dependencies, and APIs.

use_cases.title: Map Deployment Responsibilities
use_cases.description: Connect deployment files, containers, APIs, and external systems into a clearer operational picture.

prompt_templates.title: Start Repository Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a normal repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the C4 architecture workflow on this repository. Create code, component, container, and context documentation in C4-Documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Components
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for clarifying module boundaries.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this repository with the C4 workflow, then emphasize component boundaries, dependencies, interfaces, and ownership assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Document APIs and Containers
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for services and deployment architecture.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the C4 workflow to map components to containers. Include API interfaces, deployment evidence, external systems, and OpenAPI documentation where possible.

prompt_templates.title: Create Review-Ready Architecture Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete C4 documentation package for architecture review. Include code references, Mermaid diagrams, personas, user journeys, risks, and validation notes.

output_examples.input: A service repository with source code, deployment manifests, and limited README documentation.
output_examples.output: A C4 documentation folder containing code-level notes, component summaries, a container view, API descriptions, and a system context overview.

output_examples.input: A monorepo where teams need clearer ownership and dependency boundaries.
output: Component documentation grouped by logical responsibility.
Context notes that explain users and external systems.

output_examples.input: A web application with multiple APIs and infrastructure files.
output_examples.output: Container documentation that links services to deployment evidence and describes major API interfaces for review.

best_practices: Run the workflow from a clean working tree so generated documentation is easy to review.
Validate generated diagrams and API descriptions with engineers who own the system.
Exclude generated, vendored, and build directories before starting large repository analysis.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated diagrams as authoritative without source review.
Do not run the workflow on repositories that contain secrets unless access controls are clear.
Do not commit generated documentation before checking paths, links, and technical accuracy.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates C4-style code, component, container, and context documentation for a repository.

faq.question: Does it create diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow requests Mermaid diagrams for relationships, components, containers, and system context.

faq.question: Does it modify application code?
faq.answer: No. The intended output is documentation written under C4-Documentation.

faq.question: Can it document APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow requests API interface documentation and OpenAPI 3.1 specifications when APIs are found.

faq.question: Will the results be fully accurate?
faq.answer: The results depend on repository evidence and model analysis. Human review is recommended before publishing.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
